records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.402 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) passed by a grave where its occupant was being punished. He requested a palm branch, split it into two halves, and placed one at the head and the other at the feet of the grave.
It is narrated that the person in the grave was Qays ibn Fahd al-Ansari (in another narration: Qays ibn Qumayr)
When asked why he placed the branches,
He (sw) replied: "Indeed, the punishment is lessened as long as they remain green."
records.arabicText: 402 - وَ مَرَّ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ عَلَى قَبْرٍ يُعَذَّبُ صَاحِبُهُ فَدَعَا بِجَرِيدَةٍ فَشَقَّهَا نِصْفَيْنِ فَجَعَلَ وَاحِدَةً عِنْدَ رَأْسِهِ وَ اَلْأُخْرَى عِنْدَ رِجْلَيْهِ.وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ صَاحِبَ اَلْقَبْرِ كَانَ قَيْسَ بْنَ فَهْدٍ اَلْأَنْصَارِيَّ. وَ رُوِيَ: « قَيْسَ بْنَ قُمَيْرٍ » وَ أَنَّهُ قِيلَ لَهُ لِمَ وَضَعْتَهُمَا فَقَالَ «إِنَّهُ يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُ اَلْعَذَابُ مَا كَانَتَا خَضْرَاوَيْنِ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.403 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about placing the palm branch (jareedah) in the grave,
Imam (as) replied: "There is no issue (with it)”.
records.arabicText: 403 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْجَرِيدَةِ تُوضَعُ فِي اَلْقَبْرِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.404 - Ali ibn Bilal wrote to Abu Al-Hasan al-Third, Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al-Hadi (as): "A man dies in a land where there are no palm trees. Is it permissible to use something from other trees instead of the palm branch? It has been narrated from your forefathers (as) that 'the punishment is averted as long as the two palm branches remain fresh, and that it benefits both the believer and the disbeliever.'"
Imam (as) replied: "It is permissible to use fresh branches from another tree."
records.arabicText: 404 - وَ كَتَبَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ بِلاَلٍ إِلَى أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلثَّالِثِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: اَلرَّجُلُ يَمُوتُ فِي بِلاَدٍ لَيْسَ فِيهَا نَخْلٌ فَهَلْ يَجُوزُ مَكَانَ اَلْجَرِيدَةِ شَيْءٌ مِنَ اَلشَّجَرِ غَيْرِ اَلنَّخْلِ فَإِنَّهُ قَدْ رُوِيَ عَنْ آبَائِكُمْ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ «أَنَّهُ يَتَجَافَى عَنْهُ اَلْعَذَابُ مَا دَامَتِ اَلْجَرِيدَتَانِ رَطْبَتَيْنِ وَ أَنَّهَا تَنْفَعُ اَلْمُؤْمِنَ وَ اَلْكَافِرَ » فَأَجَابَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «يَجُوزُ مِنْ شَجَرٍ آخَرَ رَطْبٍ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.405 - It was narrated from Yahya ibn 'Abbad al-Makki that He said: "I heard Sufyan al-Thawri asking Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about 'al-takhdir' (placing green branches).
Imam (as) replied: 'A man from the Ansar died, and the Messenger of Allah (sw) was informed of his death. He said to those from the man's relatives who were responsible for him, "Place greenery (al-takhdir) on your companion. How few are those who will be adorned with greenery on the Day of Resurrection."'
Sufyan asked: 'What is al-takhdir?'
Imam (as) said: "A green palm branch that is placed from the base of the hands to the base of the collarbone."
records.arabicText: 405 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ عَبَّادٍ اَلْمَكِّيِّ أَنَّهُ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ سُفْيَانَ اَلثَّوْرِيَّ: يَسْأَلُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلتَّخْضِيرِ فَقَالَ «إِنَّ رَجُلاً مِنَ اَلْأَنْصَارِ هَلَكَ فَأُوذِنَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ بِمَوْتِهِ فَقَالَ لِمَنْ يَلِيهِ مِنْ قَرَابَتِهِ «خَضِّرُوا صَاحِبَكُمْ مَا أَقَلَّ اَلْمُخَضَّرِينَ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ » قَالَ وَ مَا اَلتَّخْضِيرُ فَقَالَ «جَرِيدَةٌ خَضْرَاءُ تُوضَعُ مِنْ أَصْلِ اَلْيَدَيْنِ إِلَى أَصْلِ اَلتَّرْقُوَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.405 - It was narrated from Yahya ibn 'Abbad al-Makki that He said: "I heard Sufyan al-Thawri asking Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about 'al-takhdir' (placing green branches).
Imam (as) replied: 'A man from the Ansar died, and the Messenger of Allah (sw) was informed of his death. He said to those from the man's relatives who were responsible for him,
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Place greenery (al-takhdir) on your companion. How few are those who will be adorned with greenery on the Day of Resurrection."'
Sufyan asked: 'What is al-takhdir?'
Imam (as) said: "A green palm branch that is placed from the base of the hands to the base of the collarbone."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.406 - Al-Hasan ibn Ziyad asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the palm branch that is placed with the deceased.
Imam (as) replied: "It benefits both the believer and the disbeliever”.
records.arabicText: 406 - وَ سَأَلَ اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْجَرِيدَةِ اَلَّتِي تَكُونُ مَعَ اَلْمَيِّتِ فَقَالَ «تَنْفَعُ اَلْمُؤْمِنَ وَ اَلْكَافِرَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.406 - Al-Hasan ibn Ziyad asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the palm branch that is placed with the deceased.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It benefits both the believer and the disbeliever”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.407 - Zurarah said: I asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as): "Why is a palm branch placed with the deceased?"
Imam (as) said: "The punishment and accounting (in the grave) are averted from them as long as the branch remains fresh. The entire accounting and punishment occur in a single day and a single moment—during the time it takes for the deceased to be placed in the grave and for the people to return. The two palm branches were placed for this reason, so no punishment or accounting will afflict the deceased after they have dried up, if Allah (swt) wills."
records.arabicText: 407 - وَقَالَ زُرَارَةُ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَ رَأَيْتَ اَلْمَيِّتَ إِذَا مَاتَ لِمَ تُجْعَلُ مَعَهُ اَلْجَرِيدَةُ فَقَالَ «يَتَجَافَى عَنْهُ اَلْعَذَابُ وَ اَلْحِسَابُ مَا دَامَ اَلْعُودُ رَطْباً إِنَّمَا اَلْحِسَابُ وَ اَلْعَذَابُ كُلُّهُ فِي يَوْمٍ وَاحِدٍ فِي سَاعَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ قَدْرَ مَا يُدْخَلُ اَلْقَبْرَ وَ يَرْجِعُ اَلْقَوْمُ وَ إِنَّمَا جُعِلَتِ اَلسَّعَفَتَانِ لِذَلِكَ فَلاَ يُصِيبُهُ عَذَابٌ وَ لاَ حِسَابٌ بَعْدَ جُفُوفِهِمَا إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.407 - Zurarah said: I asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Why is a palm branch placed with the deceased?"
Imam (as) said: "The punishment and accounting (in the grave) are averted from them as long as the branch remains fresh. The entire accounting and punishment occur in a single day and a single moment—during the time it takes for the deceased to be placed in the grave and for the people to return. The two palm branches were placed for this reason, so no punishment or accounting will afflict the deceased after they have dried up, if Allah (swt) wills."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.408 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Be meticulous in choosing the shrouds, for they will be resurrected in them."
records.arabicText: 408 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «تَنَوَّقُوا فِي اَلْأَكْفَانِ فَإِنَّهُمْ يُبْعَثُونَ بِهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.408 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Be meticulous in choosing the shrouds, for they will be resurrected in them."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.409 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Make the shrouds of your deceased good, for they are their adornment."
records.arabicText: 409 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَجِيدُوا أَكْفَانَ مَوْتَاكُمْ فَإِنَّهَا زِينَتُهُمْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.409 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Make the shrouds of your deceased good, for they are their adornment."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.410 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"If you shroud the deceased, and you are able to include in their shroud a garment that they used to pray in and it is clean, then do so. It is recommended that they be shrouded in what they used to pray in”.
records.arabicText: 410 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا كَفَّنْتَ اَلْمَيِّتَ فَإِنِ اِسْتَطَعْتَ أَنْ يَكُونَ فِي كَفَنِهِ ثَوْبٌ كَانَ يُصَلِّي فِيهِ نَظِيفاً فَافْعَلْ فَإِنَّهُ يُسْتَحَبُّ أَنْ يُكَفَّنَ فِيمَا كَانَ يُصَلِّي فِيهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.410 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you shroud the deceased, and you are able to include in their shroud a garment that they used to pray in and it is clean, then do so. It is recommended that they be shrouded in what they used to pray in”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.411 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Linen (الكتان) was used by the Children of Israel for shrouding, while cotton (القطن) is for the Ummah (community) of Muhammad."
records.arabicText: 411 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلْكَتَّانُ كَانَ لِبَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ يُكَفَّنُونَ بِهِ وَ اَلْقُطْنُ لِأُمَّةِ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.411 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Linen (الكتان) was used by the Children of Israel for shrouding, while cotton (القطن) is for the Ummah (community) of Muhammad."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.412 - Abu Al-Hasan the third, Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al-Hadi (as), was asked about garments made in Basra similar to Yemeni fabric, made of silk (قز) and cotton (قطن), and whether they are suitable for shrouding the deceased.
Imam (as) said: "If the cotton content is greater than the silk, then there is no issue (in using them)."
records.arabicText: 412 - وَ سُئِلَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ اَلثَّالِثُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ ثِيَابٍ تُعْمَلُ بِالْبَصْرَةِ عَلَى عَمَلِ اَلْعَصْبِ اَلْيَمَانِيِّ مِنْ قَزٍّ وَ قُطْنٍ هَلْ يَصْلُحُ أَنْ يُكَفَّنَ فِيهَا اَلْمَوْتَى فَقَالَ «إِذَا كَانَ اَلْقُطْنُ أَكْثَرَ مِنَ اَلْقَزِّ فَلاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.412 - Abu Al-Hasan the third, Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al-Hadi (as), was asked about garments made in Basra similar to Yemeni fabric, made of silk (قز) and cotton (قطن), and whether they are suitable for shrouding the deceased.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If the cotton content is greater than the silk, then there is no issue (in using them)."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.413 - Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) was asked about a man who had purchased part of the covering of the Kaaba (Kiswah) and used some of it to fulfil a need, while some of it remained with him. He was asked whether it was permissible to sell the remaining part.
Imam (as) said: "He may sell what he wants, give away what he does not need, use it, and seek its blessings."
He was then asked if it was permissible to shroud a deceased person in it
Imam (as) said: "No."
records.arabicText: 413 - وَ سُئِلَ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ رَجُلٍ اِشْتَرَى مِنْ كِسْوَةِ اَلْكَعْبَةِ شَيْئاً فَقَضَى بِبَعْضِهِ حَاجَتَهُ وَ بَقِيَ بَعْضُهُ فِي يَدِهِ هَلْ يَصْلُحُ بَيْعُهُ فَقَالَ «يَبِيعُ مَا أَرَادَ وَ يَهَبُ مَا لَمْ يُرِدْهُ وَ يَسْتَنْفِعُ بِهِ وَ يَطْلُبُ بَرَكَتَهُ» قِيلَ أَ يُكَفَّنُ فِيهِ اَلْمَيِّتُ قَالَ «لاَ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.414 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The shirt (Qamis) for the deceased should be without cuffs (غير مكفوف) and without buttons (ولا مزرر)."
records.arabicText: 414 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «يَنْبَغِي أَنْ يَكُونَ اَلْقَمِيصُ لِلْمَيِّتِ غَيْرَ مَكْفُوفٍ وَ لاَ مُزَرَّرٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.414 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The shirt (Qamis) for the deceased should be without cuffs (غير مكفوف) and without buttons (ولا مزرر)."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.415 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about whether a man should be shrouded in his shirt (Qamis).
Imam (as) said: "Remove its buttons."
I asked: "And the sleeves?"
Imam (as) said: "No, that is only necessary if it is made new for him, in which case it should have no sleeves. However, if it is an already-worn garment, only the buttons need to be removed."
records.arabicText: 415 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ اَلْقَمِيصُ أَ يُكَفَّنُ فِيهِ فَقَالَ «اِقْطَعْ أَزْرَارَهُ» قُلْتُ وَ كُمَّهُ قَالَ «لاَ إِنَّمَا ذَلِكَ إِذَا قُطِعَ لَهُ وَ هُوَ جَدِيدٌ لَمْ يُجْعَلْ لَهُ أَكْمَامٌ فَأَمَّا إِذَا كَانَ ثَوْباً لَبِيساً فَلاَ يُقْطَعُ مِنْهُ إِلاَّ اَلْأَزْرَارُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.415 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about whether a man should be shrouded in his shirt (Qamis).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Remove its buttons."
I asked: "And the sleeves?"
Imam (as) said: "No, that is only necessary if it is made new for him, in which case it should have no sleeves. However, if it is an already-worn garment, only the buttons need to be removed."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.416 - Archangel Jibril (as), brought to the Prophet Muhammad (sw) an ounce of camphor from Paradise. An ounce in this context is equivalent to forty dirhams. The Prophet (sw) divided it into three parts: one part for himself, one part for Imam Ali (as), and one part for Sayyida Fatimah Al-Zahra {S.A}.
records.arabicText: 416 - أَنَّ جَبْرَئِيلَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَتَى اَلنَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ بِأُوقِيَّةِ كَافُورٍ مِنَ اَلْجَنَّةِ وَ اَلْأُوقِيَّةُ أَرْبَعُونَ دِرْهَماً فَجَعَلَهَا اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ثَلاَثَةَ أَثْلاَثٍ ثُلُثاً لَهُ وَ ثُلُثاً لِعَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ ثُلُثاً لِفَاطِمَةَ عَلَيْهَا اَلسَّلاَمُ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.417 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever shrouds a believer, it is as if they have guaranteed their clothing until the Day of Resurrection. And whoever digs a grave for a believer, it is as if they have prepared for them a suitable dwelling until the Day of Resurrection."
records.arabicText: 417 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ كَفَّنَ مُؤْمِناً فَكَأَنَّمَا ضَمِنَ كِسْوَتَهُ إِلَى يَوْمِ اَلْقِيَامَةِ وَ مَنْ حَفَرَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ قَبْراً فَكَأَنَّمَا بَوَّأَهُ بَيْتاً مُوَافِقاً إِلَى يَوْمِ اَلْقِيَامَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.417 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever shrouds a believer, it is as if they have guaranteed their clothing until the Day of Resurrection. And whoever digs a grave for a believer, it is as if they have prepared for them a suitable dwelling until the Day of Resurrection."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.418 - Abu al-Jaroud asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who passed away: Should his nails be trimmed, his armpit hair plucked, or his pubic hair shaved if he had been bedridden for a long period?
Imam (as) said: "No."
records.arabicText: 418 - وَ سَأَلَ أَبُو اَلْجَارُودِ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُتَوَفَّى أَ تُقَلَّمُ أَظَافِيرُهُ وَ يُنْتَفُ إِبْطَاهُ وَ تُحْلَقُ عَانَتُهُ إِنْ طَالَتْ بِهِ مِنَ اَلْمَرَضِ فَقَالَ «لاَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.418 - Abu al-Jaroud asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who passed away: Should his nails be trimmed, his armpit hair plucked, or his pubic hair shaved if he had been bedridden for a long period?

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.419 - "The Prophet, was shrouded in three garments: two pieces of Yemeni cloth known as "Zafariyyah" and a garment made of cotton ‘Kursaf’ cloth."
records.arabicText: 419 - وَ كُفِّنَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فِي ثَلاَثَةِ أَثْوَابٍ فِي بُرْدَتَيْنِ ظَفِرِيَّتَيْنِ مِنْ ثِيَابِ اَلْيَمَنِ وَ ثَوْبِ كُرْسُفٍ وَ هُوَ ثَوْبُ قُطْنٍ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.420 - It is reported that the Prophet Muhammad (sw) was embalmed with a mithqal (a specific weight) of musk in addition to camphor.
records.arabicText: 420 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّهُ حُنِّطَ بِمِثْقَالِ مِسْكٍ سِوَى اَلْكَافُورِ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.421 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"My father (as) wrote in his will that I should shroud him in three garments: one of them being a Yemeni cloth (hibarah) that he used to wear for Jumu’ah prayers, along with another cloth and a shirt (Qamis)."
records.arabicText: 421 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «كَتَبَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ أَنْ أُكَفِّنَهُ فِي ثَلاَثَةِ أَثْوَابٍ أَحَدُهَا بُرْدٌ لَهُ حِبَرَةٌ كَانَ يُصَلِّي فِيهِ يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ وَ ثَوْبٌ آخَرُ وَ قَمِيصٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.421 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "My father (as) wrote in his will that I should shroud him in three garments: one of them being a Yemeni cloth (hibarah) that he used to wear for Jumu’ah prayers, along with another cloth and a shirt (Qamis)."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.422 - Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) was asked about a man who dies and whether he can be shrouded in three garments without a shirt (Qamis).
Imam (as) said: "There is no problem with that, but having a shirt is more preferred to me."
records.arabicText: 422 - وَ سُئِلَ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَمُوتُ أَ يُكَفَّنُ فِي ثَلاَثَةِ أَثْوَابٍ بِغَيْرِ قَمِيصٍ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ وَ اَلْقَمِيصُ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.422 - Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) was asked about a man who dies and whether he can be shrouded in three garments without a shirt (Qamis).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no problem with that, but having a shirt is more preferred to me."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.423 - Ammar ibn Musa al-Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a woman who dies in a state of postpartum bleeding (nifas).
Imam (as) said: "She is washed just like a pure woman is washed, as is the case for a menstruating woman or someone in a state of major ritual impurity (janabah). They are washed with one washing."
records.arabicText: 423 - وَ سَأَلَ عَمَّارُ بْنُ مُوسَى اَلسَّابَاطِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ إِذَا مَاتَتْ فِي نِفَاسِهَا كَيْفَ تُغَسَّلُ قَالَ «تُغَسَّلُ مِثْلَ مَا تُغَسَّلُ اَلطَّاهِرَةُ وَ كَذَلِكَ اَلْحَائِضُ وَ كَذَلِكَ اَلْجُنُبُ إِنَّمَا يُغَسَّلُ غُسْلاً وَاحِداً.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.423 - Ammar ibn Musa al-Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a woman who dies in a state of postpartum bleeding (nifas).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "She is washed just like a pure woman is washed, as is the case for a menstruating woman or someone in a state of major ritual impurity (janabah). They are washed with one washing."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.424 - Abu Al-Hasan al-Thalith, Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al-Hadi (as) was asked whether musk and incense (bukhoor) should be brought near the deceased.
Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
records.arabicText: 424 - وَ سُئِلَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ اَلثَّالِثُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: هَلْ يُقَرَّبُ إِلَى اَلْمَيِّتِ اَلْمِسْكُ وَ اَلْبَخُورُ قَالَ «نَعَمْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.424 - Abu Al-Hasan al-Thalith, Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al-Hadi (as) was asked whether musk and incense (bukhoor) should be brought near the deceased.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.425 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"If a woman dies in a state of postpartum bleeding (nifas) and her bleeding is heavy, She is washed up to the navel using a leather garment or something similar.
Then She is cleaned thoroughly, and cotton is placed in the front and back (private areas), and She is shrouded afterward."
records.arabicText: 425 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلْمَرْأَةُ إِذَا مَاتَتْ نُفَسَاءَ وَ كَثُرَ دَمُهَا أُدْخِلَتْ إِلَى اَلسُّرَّةِ فِي اَلْأَدَمِ أَوْ مِثْلِ اَلْأَدَمِ وَ تُنَظَّفُ ثُمَّ يُحْشَى اَلْقُبُلُ وَ اَلدُّبُرُ ثُمَّ تُكَفَّنُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.425 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If a woman dies in a state of postpartum bleeding (nifas) and her bleeding is heavy, She is washed up to the navel using a leather garment or something similar.
Then She is cleaned thoroughly, and cotton is placed in the front and back (private areas), and She is shrouded afterward."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.426 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a woman who passes away among a group of men with no male relative (mahram) present. Should they wash her while She remains clothed?
Imam (as) replied: "If they do that, it will be burdensome for them. Rather, they should wash only her hands."
records.arabicText: 426 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ تَمُوتُ مَعَ رِجَالٍ لَيْسَ مَعَهُمْ ذُو مَحْرَمٍ هَلْ يُغَسِّلُونَهَا وَ عَلَيْهَا ثِيَابُهَا فَقَالَ «إِذاً يُدْخَلَ ذَلِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَ لَكِنْ يَغْسِلُونَ كَفَّيْهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.426 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a woman who passes away among a group of men with no male relative (mahram) present. Should they wash her while She remains clothed?
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If they do that, it will be burdensome for them. Rather, they should wash only her hands."
